summary: Super-admin in ServiceAlert operates on behalf of any customer in the system. Rather than being scoped to one customer (like a normal admin), super-admin pages show data for a chosen context: country → customer → (optionally) profile. This context is selected explicitly via a shared selector bar that appears on all super-admin pages. Normal admin has no such selector — context comes implicitly from the logged-in user's own customer and active profile.

user_roles.description: A user of a customer (e.g. GreenAi employee using ServiceAlert as a customer). Operates within their own customer only. Active profile is selected in the global app-shell once per session.
A GreenAi employee with elevated access. Can administer any customer in the system. Must explicitly select which customer context to work in on each super-admin page.
user_roles.note: A super-admin also has their own customer context (GreenAi) and uses the normal admin section for that. Only the /admin/super/* area requires explicit context selection.

context_selector.description: A shared UI component rendered at the top of all super-admin pages. Provides cascading dropdowns for country → customer → profile. Persists selection in scoped state during the super-admin session.

levels.name: profile
levels.required: conditional — required on profile-scoped pages, optional on customer-scoped pages

example_pages: UNKNOWN — no confirmed example in route analysis

context_selector.persistence: context selection persists across navigation within super-admin section — navigating from /admin/super/users to /admin/super/customers retains country+customer selection
context_selector.deep_link: context encoded as query parameters for bookmarkable URLs: ?countryId=X&customerId=Y&profileId=Z

shell_elements: global profile dropdown (profile selector — sets active profile for entire session)
left sidebar or top nav for admin sections
normal_admin_layout.per_page_context: none — page reads context from app state (current customer + active profile)

shell_elements: global profile dropdown (for super-admin's own GreenAi profile — separate from work context)
super-admin context selector bar (country + customer + profile dropdowns)
left sidebar or top nav for super-admin sections
super_admin_layout.per_page_context: explicit — pages receive context from SuperAdminContextState

state_service.name: SuperAdminContextState
events: OnContextChanged — fired when any selector changes, pages subscribe to reload data

context_selector_component.name: SuperAdminContextSelector
context_selector_component.behavior: cascading dropdowns — each level loads options based on parent selection

option_a.name: query-parameter encoding
option_a.pros: clean page URLs, context easily shareable, deep-linking works
option_a.cons: context must be extracted from URL on every page load

option_b.name: route segment encoding
option_b.pros: context always visible in URL, strict deep-link
option_b.cons: longer URLs, segments add to route complexity

route_strategy.recommendation: option_a (query parameters) — consistent with how most admin tools handle cross-cutting context

page_pattern.description: Each super-admin page injects SuperAdminContextState, subscribes to OnContextChanged, and loads data based on current context. Pages must handle 'no context selected' gracefully.
skeleton: Reload(): fetch data using Context.SelectedCustomerId etc.

normal_admin_vs_super_admin_comparison.description: Same functional capability (e.g. 'manage users') exists in both admin and super-admin but with different context handling

admin_version.context: always the logged-in user's own customer + active profile
admin_version.data_scope: users belonging to my customer only

super_admin_version.context: selected customer from super-admin context selector
super_admin_version.context_selector: country + customer dropdowns visible on page

normal_admin_vs_super_admin_comparison.blazor_implication: These are likely the SAME Blazor component parameterized differently — not two separate components. The component receives customerId as a parameter; the source (app state vs context selector) is the layout's concern.

unknowns: Which specific super-admin pages require only country+customer (no profile) vs country+customer+profile — not determinable from route analysis alone
Whether the context selector persists across browser refresh or resets on reload — session vs localStorage
Whether super-admin can impersonate a user/profile context or only observe data for that context
Whether 'country' is a true first-class entity or just a filter convenience (e.g. are there country-level settings?)
Whether the global profile dropdown (super-admin's own GreenAi profile) and the super-admin context selector are ever in conflict — e.g. can a super-admin select GreenAi as the work context?
